item.title: Skill vetting bypass via comment-wrapped payloads and weak encoded-payload severity
item.intent: Ensure no malicious skill can pass vetting by hiding code in comments or encoding. Vetting must use language-aware parsing, not line-by-line heuristics.
item.description: Code review found two issues in src/evolve/skills/vet.ts: (1) Comment detection uses simple line-start heuristics that miss multi-line comments, here-docs, and Python triple-quoted strings. (2) Encoded payloads (base64, hex) are flagged as medium severity but buildSummary() only rejects critical/high, so obfuscated malicious code passes vetting.
steps: Replace line-by-line comment detection with proper language-aware parsing
Bump encoded_payload and obfuscation_patterns severity from medium to high
Add test cases for comment-wrapped payloads, here-docs, and triple-quoted strings
acceptanceCriteria: Skill vetting uses language-aware comment parsing, not line-start heuristics
Encoded payload and obfuscation pattern findings are classified as high severity
Skills containing base64-encoded shell commands are rejected by vetting
Skills hiding payloads in multi-line comments or here-docs are detected
